_instructions: Copy this file to email_data_mmm_yyyy.json and populate during the run. Delete after successful email send. All string values must be plain text (no Unicode above U+007F). HTML sub-tags allowed in 'paragraphs' arrays: <strong>, <em>, <a href>, <code>, <span style>. Do not include <head>, <style>, or block-level tags in strings. PIPELINE-AWARE SECTIONS: s3_investment_cases and s5_watchlist items carry a 'pipeline' field (growth_stock | vci | energy) that controls rendering. The template includes example schemas for all three paths — use the matching schema for each action. Items prefixed '_schema_note' are documentation only and are ignored by build_monthly_isa_email.py.

s1_decision_summary.intro: One-sentence summary of this month's decision.

actions.name: Adobe Inc (ADBE)
actions.timing: This week — obtain Citi preclearance first
actions.note: Limit order ~$207 USD. Preclearance valid 2 trading days. 30-day hold applies.

actions.name: Marks & Spencer (MKS)
actions.timing: Day 31 from April purchase — verify date

s1_decision_summary.net_effect: Stock sleeve rises from £X (X%) to ~£Y (~Y%) post-trade.

s3_investment_cases.name: Adobe Inc
paragraphs: Thesis: Adobe's valuation has compressed on AI fears...
Trade mechanics: ~21 shares at limit ~$207 USD...
Downside case: Revenue growth decelerates to 5% -- fair value ~$180 USD (~13% downside).

s3_investment_cases.name: Marks & Spencer
paragraphs: Original thesis: UK consumer recovery + M&S digital transformation.
Thesis break: April 2026 cyberattack -- ~£300M FY2026 profit impact.
Decision: Sell all 620 shares. Unrealised loss ~£122 (-5.5%). Proceeds to cash for June deployment.

s3_investment_cases._schema_note: PATH B (VCI ASYMMETRIC) — use this structure when action is a VCI candidate. No Part A/B tables. ACS summary + NVIDIA signals + T1-T7 status.
s3_investment_cases.name: [Company Name]

t1_t7_status.T1: CLEAR — no credible bottleneck bypass identified
t1_t7_status.T2: CLEAR — CEO/founder intact, platform vision unchanged
t1_t7_status.T5: CLEAR — no competing technology at production scale
t1_t7_status.T6: CLEAR — no regulatory market removal risk
t1_t7_status.T7: CLEAR — no paradigm shift undermining platform

paragraphs: Asymmetric thesis: [Claude fills — VCI B1-B12 narrative, NVIDIA signals, structural bottleneck, platform inflection catalyst]
Entry and sizing: [Claude fills — 0.5-1.5% ISA per 2b outcome, limit price, ACS threshold for add/trim, max loss tolerance]
Thesis monitoring: [Claude fills — T1-T7 conditions, NVIDIA signal count to watch, next catalyst date]
Portfolio fit: [Claude fills — asymmetric sleeve budget remaining, Citigroup overlap, correlation to fund sleeve]
Execution: [Claude fills — shares, limit price, dealing cost, FX, preclearance reminder, 30-day hold]

s3_investment_cases._schema_note: PATH C (ENERGY/CLEAN-TECH) — use this structure for energy, renewables, or clean-tech stocks. Scorecard is /36 (not /54). Note: only the energy_screener.py thresholds apply.
s3_investment_cases.name: [Energy Company Name]
paragraphs: Investment thesis: [Claude fills — energy transition angle, contracted vs merchant exposure, data centre / AI demand catalyst, capacity growth plan]
Valuation framing: [Claude fills — EV/EBITDA vs sector peers, commodity price deck assumptions, capex cycle stage, base/bull/bear cases]
Portfolio fit: [Claude fills — energy sleeve allocation, currency, sector diversification vs existing holdings, Citigroup overlap]
Execution: [Claude fills — shares, limit price, dealing cost, FX, preclearance reminder, 30-day hold]

items.earliest_sale: Day 31 from April purchase -- verify date

s4_liquidation_tracker.notes: No other liquidation candidates active. VUAG/Vanguard US Eq Idx overlap flagged for June review.

items._schema_note: PATH B (VCI) watchlist item — score shows ACS/100
items.name: [VCI Candidate]
items.action_trigger: 2a: Thesis [intact/strengthening] — [NVIDIA signals 5/6 active]. 2b: Price $[X] vs entry $[Y] — [at/above entry by X%] — [full/reduced size outcome].

items._schema_note: PATH C (energy) watchlist item — score shows X/36
items.name: [Energy Company]
items.action_trigger: Entry on pullback to $[Y]. Commodity price sensitivity: [note].

s5_watchlist.excluded: MORN, BR, FSG, PGHN -- Financial Services sector; excluded per Citi external exposure constraint.

detail_items.name: Paylocity
paragraphs: HR + payroll SaaS for US mid-market employers (50--5,000 employees). Fwd P/E ~12.2x, Rev CAGR ~15%, NRR ~110%, GM ~65%, FCF positive.

detail_items._schema_note: PATH B (VCI) detail item — no Part A/B metrics. ACS summary + T1-T7 status.
detail_items.name: [VCI Candidate]
paragraphs: ACS [X]/100 | NVIDIA-class: [Yes/No] | Signals: [N]/6 active ([list active signals]). Bottleneck: [one-sentence description]. VCI run: [date].
T1-T7 status: all clear. Next catalyst: [named event, date]. Entry level: $[Y] (2b outcome: [full/reduced size if above entry]).

detail_items._schema_note: PATH C (energy) detail item — energy scorecard /36.
detail_items.name: [Energy Company]
paragraphs: Energy scorecard [X]/36 (Part A [A]/20 | Part B [B]/16). [One-sentence thesis: sector, contracted revenue %, AI demand angle]. EBITDA margin [X]% | EV/EBITDA [Y]x | CapEx [Z]% of revenue.
Commodity sensitivity: [note on oil/gas/power price exposure]. Entry level: $[Y]. Action trigger: [price or catalyst condition].

holdings.name: Artemis SmartGARP European Eq I Acc

s6_portfolio_snapshot.performance_header: Portfolio Performance vs Benchmark (Global Large-Cap Blend Equity)
s6_portfolio_snapshot.risk_metrics: Sharpe ratio X.XX | Beta X.XX | Alpha XX.XX | R2 X.XX.

s7_stock_sleeve.notes: Note on Scottish Mortgage (SMT): Classified as a fund for sleeve purposes -- not counted in stock sleeve %.

step8a_summary.regime: REGIME: Expansion -- Watch: Fed rate cut timeline.

s8_fund_review.xray_summary: Technology 28.21% vs 27.27% bmark (within band). Healthcare 7.36% vs 10.84% -- underweight -3.48pp (flagged).
s8_fund_review._step8a_summary_note: Key is named 'step8a_summary' for backward compatibility with build_monthly_isa_email.py. This section corresponds to Step 6 outputs in the current run context. Do not rename this key without also updating build_monthly_isa_email.py.

items.title: AI infrastructure capex
paragraphs: Hyperscaler capex: MSFT, GOOGL, META, AMZN guidance remains robust for H1 2026. Directly drives AVGO custom silicon (XPUs) and networking revenue.

items.title: US Federal Reserve
paragraphs: No rate cuts expected near-term (Jun 2026 consensus). USD/GBP ~1.36. US stock sleeve denominated in USD -- FX exposure ~12% rising to ~15% post-ADBE. Accepted risk over 10--12yr horizon.

s11_retrospective._note: 1-5 items. Each item MUST have: title, problem, action, category (accuracy|completeness|efficiency|effectiveness), impact (XL|L|M|S|XS).

items.title: Portfolio change tracking gap
items.problem: Six positions were sold and four new positions opened between the April 4 baseline and the May 1 run date. None of this was logged in advance -- all changes had to be reverse-engineered from the xlsx file comparison.
items.action: Create a running project_isa_trades_log.md memory file and log each trade (date, ticker, shares, price, rationale) at the point of execution.

items.title: MKS thesis not formally documented at purchase
items.problem: The original thesis for MKS was reconstructed from context, not from a written record. Thesis-break conditions could not be checked at point of event.
items.action: Use the trades log to record thesis, expected holding period, and thesis-break conditions at point of purchase for every stock added.

items.title: Retrospective missing impact category and magnitude
items.problem: Prior retrospective items did not include impact category (accuracy/completeness/efficiency/effectiveness) or impact rating (XL/L/M/S/XS) as specified in the Run_Context.
items.action: All retrospective items now include category badge and impact rating badge via the pre-built email script.
